content:
{
  "title": "Cross-Lingual Bridge Prompting: Leveraging High-Resource Language Knowledge to Enhance LLMs on Low-Resource Tasks",
  "problem": "Large language models exhibit reduced performance on low-resource or underrepresented languages, especially in complex tasks requiring reasoning or domain expertise.",
  "motivation": "Despite advances in multilingual modeling, performance gaps persist for low-resource languages due to limited training data and suboptimal transfer from high-resource languages. Addressing this is crucial for equitable NLP advancements and for supporting global linguistic diversity.",
  "method": "We propose Cross-Lingual Bridge Prompting (CLBP), a method that explicitly integrates high-resource language context into prompts for low-resource tasks. Given an input in a low-resource language, CLBP first constructs a parallel or analogous context in a related high-resource language (using translation or aligned examples) and provides both contexts as part of the prompt to the LLM. The prompt structure encourages the LLM to map task-relevant reasoning or knowledge from the high-resource context to the low-resource one in a controlled, contrastive format. This bridging approach is designed to leverage the LLM's stronger capabilities in high-resource languages while grounding outputs in the low-resource linguistic context.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate CLBP on multilingual benchmarks such as XNLI and FLORES-101, focusing on low-resource language pairs. Baselines include standard prompting, translate-test, and retrieval-augmented prompting. Metrics will include task accuracy, BLEU scores (for generation), and cross-lingual transfer effectiveness. Ablations will test the impact of bridge context fidelity and structure."
}
